title: 10 best herbal creams for stomach discomfort

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with stomach discomfort because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. This helps to soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the stomach, alleviating symptoms such as nausea and cramps. The warming sensation of ginger also stimulates digestion and circulation, allowing for a faster recovery from stomach discomfort. Additionally, ginger has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the stomach lining from further irritation.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate stomach discomfort.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea and vomiting.
Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help protect the stomach lining and reduce the risk of stomach ulcers.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most used part due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe stomach discomfort.
Roots: Roots are often used as an alternative to rhyzomes, as they contain similar compounds that aid in reducing nausea and stomach pain.
Buds: Buds are used for their high volatile oil content, which helps to reduce inflammation and provide relief from stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather 200g of dried zingiber officinale roots and 100g of distilled water for the infusion process.
Boil the distilled water in a saucepan for 10 minutes to create a sterilized environment for infusion.
Steep the dried zingiber officinale roots in the boiled water for 20 minutes to extract the active compounds.
Mix 50g of shea butter and 20g of coconut oil in a separate container for blending purposes.
Blend the infused zingiber officinale liquid with the shea butter and coconut oil mixture using a hand blender.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with stomach discomfort because they contain compounds that soothe and calm the digestive tract. The anti-inflammatory properties of licorice root, the primary ingredient in these creams, reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach and intestines. This leads to a decrease in symptoms such as bloating, nausea, and cramps. Additionally, the creams' ability to protect the mucous membranes in the stomach helps to prevent further irritation and discomfort, promoting a faster recovery and relief from stomach issues.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Saponins: They help with stomach discomfort by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, soothing ulcers, and protecting the mucous lining.
Glycyrrhizin: It helps alleviate stomach discomfort by inhibiting the production of stomach acid, which reduces inflammation and ulcers in the digestive tract.

Flavonoids (including Isoliquiritigenin and Licochalcone A): They help with stomach discomfort by possessing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which protect the digestive tract from damage and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that helps to soothe stomach discomfort.
Barks: They are used to extract licorice extract, which helps to calm stomach ulcers and soothe digestive issues.
Leaves: They are used to prepare licorice tea, which has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help to alleviate stomach discomfort and digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Extract 100g of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra with 500ml of hot water at 90 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl to remove the roots.
Combine 50g of the extracted liquid with 50g of a moisturizing base such as beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler at 60-70 degrees celsius for 10-15 minutes until the beeswax is fully incorporated.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken before transferring it to a clean container for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams helps with stomach discomfort because they contain curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound derived from the turmeric root. Curcumin works by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of stomach discomfort such as bloating, cramps, and nausea. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant properties help protect the stomach lining from damage, promoting a healthy digestive environment. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Helps with stomach discomfort by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes that contribute to stomach pain and irritation.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): Assists in alleviating stomach discomfort by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation.

Bisdemethoxycurcumin (BDMC): Contributes to the relief of stomach discomfort by displaying potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help soothe and protect the stomach from irritation and damage.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes: The underground stems of Curcuma longa, which contain curcumin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and digestive properties.
Roots: Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa are used for their medicinal properties, including reducing inflammation and soothing digestive issues.
Stems: Stems: The stems of Curcuma longa contain bioactive compounds that help to ease stomach discomfort and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Melt 1 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 5-7 minutes.
Add 2 teaspoons of dried curcuma longa powder and stir for 2 minutes.
Combine 1/2 cup of shea butter and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a heatproof bowl.
Whip the mixture for 5 minutes using an electric mixer until smooth and fluffy.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized container and allow it to cool for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare creams helps with stomach discomfort because they contain active compounds like anethole, limonene, and fenchone. These components have anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, which help to soothe and calm the digestive system. The creams can also aid in reducing spasms and cramps, as well as relaxing the muscles in the stomach. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of Foeniculum vulgare creams can help to alleviate digestive issues caused by bacterial overgrowth, making them a popular remedy for stomach discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Furanocoumarins: They help alleviate stomach discomfort by inhibiting the growth of bacteria that can cause gastrointestinal issues.
Phenolic compounds: They possess anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the stomach and alleviate discomfort.
Terpenes (specifically, anethole): They exhibit carminative properties, helping to ease digestive issues by reducing gas and bloating in the stomach.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are used due to their high content of volatile oils, particularly anethole, which has anti-inflammatory and carminative properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their high content of fenchone and anethole, which have anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic effects to soothe stomach discomfort.
Stems: The stems are used for their content of volatile oils, particularly anethole, which helps to relax the muscles in the stomach and provide relief from cramps and spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers at the peak of their fragrance and store them in a dry container for later use.
Combine 20g of dried foeniculum vulgare with 100ml of jojoba oil in a double boiler and heat for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then add 10ml of vitamin e oil.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it becomes smooth and creamy then add 5g of beeswax.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams helps with stomach discomfort because of its unique alkaloid composition, particularly quinine and quinidine, which possess anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. These compounds help to calm irritated tissues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate digestive issues. The creams also have a local anesthetic effect, which can help to numb the pain and discomfort associated with stomach problems, providing quick relief and promoting a sense of well-being. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to provide relief from various stomach issues.
remedy_constituents: Quinoline Alkaloids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce stomach discomfort and alleviate cramps.
Hydroxyanthraquinones: These compounds have a soothing effect on the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and helping to alleviate symptoms of stomach discomfort.
Flavonoids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, thus alleviating stomach discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Cinchona officinalis are the most commonly used part to make creams for stomach discomfort, as they contain quinine and other alkaloids that help alleviate symptoms.
Barks: The barks of Cinchona officinalis are also used, as they contain high amounts of quinine and other active compounds that help treat stomach issues and other health problems.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Cinchona officinalis are sometimes used, as they contain lower amounts of active compounds compared to leaves and barks, but still provide some benefits for treating stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 20g of dried cinchona officinalis bark in 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and discard the solids then add 10g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter.
Melt the beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot.
Add 20ml of distilled water and 5ml of glycerin to the melted mixture and stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify completely at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams helps with stomach discomfort because of its potent anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. The menthol in peppermint oil relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramps and spasms. Additionally, mentha x piperita creams stimulate digestive enzymes, improving nutrient absorption and reducing bloating. Its calming effect on the stomach also helps to alleviate nausea and heartburn, providing quick relief from stomach discomfort and promoting a sense of well-being. This makes it a popular natural remedy for digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps with stomach discomfort by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the stomach, which can ease cramps and spasms.
Caffeic acid: Contributes to stomach comfort by reducing inflammation and preventing the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause digestive issues.
Limonene: Provides relief from stomach discomfort by reducing inflammation, relaxing the stomach muscles, and improving digestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their digestive and anti-inflammatory properties, peppermint leaves are commonly used to soothe stomach discomfort and reduce nausea.
Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita are also used, as they contain menthol, which helps to ease digestion and alleviate stomach cramps.
Roots: Peppermint roots are sometimes used, as they contain menthone, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and may help to reduce stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and stems from a pesticide-free garden at dawn.
Infuse 1 cup of the harvested leaves and stems in 2 cups of boiling distilled water for 10 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter with the herbal liquid in a double boiler at 160°f.
Whip the cooled mixture with an electric mixer until it becomes a smooth and creamy texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with stomach discomfort because of its anti-inflammatory properties and soothing effects on the digestive tract. The cream's gel-like substance contains compounds that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach and intestines. It can also help calm digestive issues such as bloating, cramps, and nausea. Additionally, the cream's hydrating properties can aid in healing and protecting the stomach lining, promoting a sense of relief and comfort for those experiencing stomach discomfort. This natural remedy can be a gentle and effective solution for stomach issues.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach, providing relief from discomfort.
Aliphatic glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and protective effects on the gastric mucosa, helping to prevent damage and alleviate symptoms associated with stomach discomfort.

Furanochromones: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation, prevent oxidative stress, and promote healing in the stomach, ultimately providing relief from discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are most commonly used due to their rich gel content, which is extracted and used to soothe stomach discomfort.
Stems: The stems of Aloe barbadensis contain a high concentration of anthraquinones, which helps to relax the muscles and alleviate stomach cramps.
Roots: The roots are used to add a calming effect to stomach discomfort creams, due to their anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe irritated tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200ml of fresh aloe barbadensis gel from mature leaves with thick fleshy interiors.
Weigh out 20g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter for the moisturizing base.
Melt the beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler at a low heat for 15 minutes.
Combine 20ml of distilled water with 2g of vitamin e oil and add it to the melted base.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with stomach discomfort because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the digestive system. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba work together to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach, providing relief from cramps, bloating, and other digestive issues. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's ability to improve blood flow and reduce oxidative stress helps to promote a healthy gut environment, further alleviating stomach discomfort and promoting overall digestive well-being. Regular use may lead to improved digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help to reduce inflammation and protect the stomach lining from damage, thereby alleviating stomach discomfort.
Terpenoids: Possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and calm the stomach, reducing discomfort and inflammation.
Bilobalide: Exhibits a sedative effect on the stomach, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to stomach discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe stomach discomfort.
Seeds: Used due to their ability to regulate digestive enzymes and reduce inflammation, providing relief from stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 10 grams of ginkgo biloba dried extract and add to a stainless steel mixing bowl.
Combine 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a separate stainless steel mixing bowl.
Heat the beeswax and coconut oil mixture over low heat for 5 minutes to melt completely.
Add the ginkgo biloba extract to the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture and stir to combine thoroughly.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let cool at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber zerumbet creams helps with stomach discomfort because they contain the active compound zerumbone, which has anti-inflammatory properties. This helps to soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation that can cause discomfort. The cream's warming sensation also helps to relax the stomach muscles, allowing for better digestion and reducing symptoms of nausea and bloating. Additionally, zerumbone has been shown to have antispasmodic effects, which can help to calm stomach cramps and spasms, providing relief from stomach discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Zerumbone: A sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the stomach and alleviate discomfort.

Curcuminoids: A group of phenolic compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the stomach lining, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of stomach discomfort.

Gingerols: A group of phenolic compounds with anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties, which can help reduce inflammation, relax the muscles in the stomach, and alleviate symptoms of stomach discomfort such as nausea and cramping.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Used due to their high content of gingerol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate stomach discomfort.
Leaves: Used due to their rich source of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can soothe stomach issues.
Roots: Used due to their high concentration of sesquiterpenes and phenolic compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of the rhizome of zingiber zerumbet at dawn when the dew is still present.
Steam 10-15 grams of the rhizome for 30 minutes to extract its volatile compounds and oils.
Mix the steamed rhizome with 50 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil.
Add 10-15 drops of vitamin e oil to the mixture to enhance its shelf life and stability.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify completely before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zanthoxylum bungeanum creams helps with stomach discomfort because of its unique blend of bioactive compounds, including coumarins and alkaloids. These natural ingredients have been traditionally used to soothe and calm digestive issues, such as bloating and cramps. The anti-inflammatory properties of Zanthoxylum bungeanum creams can also help reduce inflammation in the stomach, promoting a healthy gut environment. Furthermore, its analgesic properties can alleviate pain and discomfort associated with stomach issues, providing relief and promoting overall digestive well-being.
remedy_constituents: Chalcones: Chalcones have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the stomach and alleviate discomfort.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids possess antioxidant properties that help protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress and damage, thus reducing discomfort.
Coumarins: Coumarins have a calming effect on the stomach and intestines, helping to reduce spasms, cramps, and other symptoms of stomach discomfort.
remedy_parts: Barks: Used to make creams due to its anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate stomach discomfort.
Fruits: Used to make creams because its extracts possess antispasmodic properties that help soothe stomach cramps and discomfort.
Leaves: Used to make creams as its extracts contain compounds that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate stomach pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30g of dried zanthoxylum bungeanum leaves and 20g of beeswax for cream base preparation.
Infuse 30g of dried zanthoxylum bungeanum leaves in 100ml of coconut oil for 2 hours at 60 degrees celsius.
Combine 30g of infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes or until beeswax is fully melted.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and store it in an airtight container for later use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for stomach discomfort are topical creams infused with plant extracts that help alleviate digestive issues by reducing inflammation and soothing the stomach lining. The best herbal creams for stomach discomfort include Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Curcuma longa, Foeniculum vulgare, Cinchona officinalis, Mentha x piperita, Aloe barbadensis, Ginkgo biloba, Zingiber zerumbet, and Zanthoxylum bungeanum. If left untreated, stomach discomfort can lead to chronic pain, digestive problems, and fatigue, negatively impacting daily life, work, and relationships.
